note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Held-out validation should show the merger agreement terminated rather than merely delayed.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out March 4 company and news evidence showed JetBlue and Spirit terminated the merger agreement because regulatory obstacles made timely closing unlikely, validating merger optionality collapse as the replay winner.

rows.expected_observable: Future disclosures should emphasize refinancing, liquidity, or profitability actions.
rows.label: partial
rows.what_happened: Spirit did discuss standalone initiatives, refinancing, and profitability steps, but the validation source was still anchored to termination of the merger path.

rows.expected_observable: The narrative should weaken if the companies terminate the agreement because closing conditions cannot be met.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: The companies terminated the agreement rather than preserving a live appeal-led deal path, weakening the appeal optionality explanation.

rows.expected_observable: Other airline merger targets or peers should show similar abnormal pressure if this narrative dominates.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: The validation fixture keeps broader airline regulatory read-through pending because the held-out evidence was specific to JetBlue and Spirit.
